title: NSDAP Foreign Policy
description: The foreign policy objectives and achievements of the Nazi regime, 1933–1939.
objectives: Expansion into Lebensraum in Eastern Europe
Destruction of the Soviet Union and 'Jewish Bolshevism'
Establishment of German hegemony over Europe

milestones.event: Germany withdraws from the League of Nations and the World Disarmament Conference.
Anglo-German Naval Agreement — Britain accepts German naval expansion. Saar returned to Germany by plebiscite. Nuremberg Laws enacted.
Remilitarisation of the Rhineland. Rome-Berlin Axis proclaimed. Anti-Comintern Pact with Japan signed.
Anschluss with Austria (March). Sudetenland crisis and Munich Agreement (September) — Britain and France concede Sudetenland.

Occupation of remainder of Czechoslovakia (March) in violation of Munich. Pact of Steel with Italy (May). Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact with Soviet Union (August) — secret protocol divides Eastern Europe. Invasion of Poland (September) — World War II begins.
